dialogueChunks.intro: {player_name}, have you noticed the sky over {location}? That sickly green light isn't natural. Nothing about what's happening here is natural.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: The land itself is turning against us. Crops blacken overnight, wells taste of copper, and the animals won't come within a mile of the old temple {location_hint}. {target_npc} says the spirits that guard this region have been angered: something disturbed the shrine deep in the temple, and now the curse is spreading. We need {quest_item} to perform a cleansing ritual before the corruption reaches the settlement. The {enemy_type} that have been spawning near the temple are a symptom, not the cause.
dialogueChunks.outro: I don't pretend to understand the divine. But I understand dying land when I see it, and ours is dying fast.

playerResponses.text: Cursed temples and angry spirits. Not my usual work, but I'll go.
Sounds like superstition. But if the land really is dying, I'll investigate.
I don't meddle with forces I don't understand. Find a priest.

dialogueChunks.intro: Don't go near the water, {player_name}. Please. Not until you hear what I have to say.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: Three days ago the river through {location} turned black. Not dirty: black, like ink. {target_npc} tested it and the results were terrifying: anything living that touches it withers within hours. The alchemists believe an ancient shrine upstream was desecrated: someone broke the seals that kept the corruption contained. I've studied the old texts, and the only way to purify the source is with {quest_item}, applied directly to the shrine stones. The path upstream is thick with {enemy_type} drawn to the dark energy.
dialogueChunks.outro: Whatever was sealed in that shrine is awake now. If we don't put it back to sleep, this entire valley will be poisoned.

playerResponses.text: Show me the old texts. I'll find the shrine and cleanse it.
Desecrated shrines and cursed rivers. What have you people been doing out here?
Ancient corruption is beyond my abilities. You need a scholar, not a sword.

dialogueChunks.intro: I've traveled far to reach {location}, {player_name}, because I heard there's someone here brave enough or foolish enough to help me challenge what no mortal should.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: The spirit guardians of the old forest {location_hint} have turned hostile. For generations they protected travelers and kept the {enemy_type} at bay. Now they attack anyone who enters. {target_npc} ventured in last week to leave the traditional offerings and barely escaped alive. Something has corrupted them: twisted their purpose from protection to destruction. I believe {quest_item} can anchor a purification ward, but someone has to get close enough to the heart-tree to place it.
dialogueChunks.outro: We're not fighting a mortal enemy. We're trying to heal something sacred that's been wounded. That takes a different kind of courage.

playerResponses.text: Healing what's sacred sounds like a cause worth risking for.
Corrupted spirits, cursed forests, certain danger. Why not.
I respect the old spirits enough to stay out of their business.
